blocks:
(١) الرَّحْمنُ. (1) The All-beneficent. (٢) عَلَّمَ الْقُرْآنَ. (2) taught the Quran. (٣) خَلَقَ الْإِنْسانَ. (3) He created man. (٤) عَلَّمَهُ الْبَيانَ ** (4) He taught him articulation. It was said: Since this surah encompasses the enumeration of worldly (الدنيويّة) and otherworldly (الاخرويّة) blessings, He began it with ** الرَّحْمنُ (The All-beneficent). And He prioritized the most magnificent and noblest of blessings, which is the teaching of the Quran, for it is the foundation of the religion (الدّين), the origin of the Divine Law (الشرع), the greatest revelation (الوحي), and the most cherished of books (الكتب). Since, by virtue of its miraculous nature (اعجازه) and its inclusion of their essence (خلاصتها), it is a confirmer of itself and of them. Then He followed it with the blessing of creating man and granting him that by which he is distinguished from the rest of the animals (الحيوان): namely, expressing what is in the conscience (الضّمير) and making others understand what he has perceived.

And in al-Majma', al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: الْبَيانَ ** (articulation) is the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم) by which everything was known.

(٥) الشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ بِحُسْبانٍ ** (5) The sun and the moon [move] by a reckoning. They both run according to a known, predetermined calculation in their constellations (بروجهما) and stations (منازلهما). And by that, the affairs of the created beings (الكائنات) are regulated, the seasons (الفصول) and times (الأوقات) vary, and the years (السنون) are known. (٦) وَالنَّجْمُ ** (6) The stars and the trees prostrate. The plant (النبات) which emerges (يَنْجُم)—that is, sprouts (يَطْلُع)—from the earth and has no stalk (ساق). ** وَالشَّجَرُ ** The one which has a stalk (ساق). They both prostrate (يَسْجُدانِ), meaning they both submit (يَنْقادانِ) to Allah in what He wills for them naturally (طبعاً), a submission like the prostration of the accountable ones (المكلّفين) willingly (طوعاً). (٧) وَالسَّماءَ رَفَعَها ** (7) He raised the heaven and set up the balance. He created it raised, both in location (محلّا) and rank (مرتبة), for it is the source (منشأ) of His decrees (أقضيته), the place where His commands (أحكامه) descend (متنزّل), and the abode (محلّ) of His angels (ملائكته). ** وَوَضَعَ الْمِيزانَ ** The justice (العدل), by granting fully to every prepared (مستعدّ) being what is due to it (مستحقّه), and by fulfilling the right (حقّه) of every rightful owner (ذي حقّ), until the affair of the world (العالم) became regulated and upright, just as he صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By justice (العدل) the heavens (السّماوات) and the earth (الأرض) were established (قامت)."

(٨) أَلَّا تَطْغَوْا فِي الْمِيزانِ ** (8) That you may not violate the balance, So that you do not transgress in it (فِيهِ), meaning: do not exceed the bounds (لَا تَعْتَدُوا) and do not go beyond fairness (الْإِنْصَاف). (٩) وَأَقِيمُوا الْوَزْنَ بِالْقِسْطِ وَلا تُخْسِرُوا الْمِيزانَ ** (9) and maintain the weighing with justice, and do not diminish the balance. And do not diminish it (وَلَا تَنْقُصُوهُ), for it is its right that it be made level (يُسَوَّى), because it is the purpose (الْمَقْصُود) of its establishment (وَضْعِهِ). (١٠) وَالْأَرْضَ وَضَعَها ** (10) And the earth He has set for the creatures. He preserved it (حَفِظَهَا) spread out (مَدْحُوَّة) for the creatures (لِلْأَنَامِ), meaning: for creation (لِلْخَلْقِ). (١١) فِيها فاكِهَةٌ ** (11) In it are fruits, Varieties of what is enjoyed (يُتَفَكَّهُ بِهِ), وَالنَّخْلُ ذَاتُ الْأَكْمَامِ (and the date-palms with sheaths), meaning: the containers of the dates (أَوْعِيَةُ التَّمْرِ). (١٢) وَالْحَبُ ** (12) and grain with the husk, And the fruit (الثَّمَرَة) like wheat (الْحِنْطَة), barley (الشَّعِير), and the rest of what is used for sustenance (يُتَغَذَّى بِهِ). ذُو الْعَصْفِ (with the husk) means: possessing dry leaves (الْوَرَقِ الْيَابِسِ) like straw (كَالتِّبْنِ). وَالرَّيْحَانُ (and fragrant herbs) means: the fragrant (الْمَشْمُوم) sustenance (الرِّزْق), derived from their saying: "I went out seeking the sustenance (رَيْحَان) of Allah."

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Regarding الرَّحْمَنُ عَلَّمَ الْقُرْآنَ (The All-beneficent taught the Quran), He said: Allah taught the Quran. It was asked: خَلَقَ الْإِنْسَانَ (He created man)? He said: That is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). It was asked: عَلَّمَهُ الْبَيَانَ (Taught him the articulation)? He said: He taught him the articulation (بَيَان) of everything that people need. It was asked: الشَّمْسُ وَالْقَمَرُ بِحُسْبَانٍ (The sun and the moon follow a reckoning)? He said: They are subject to the punishment (عَذَاب) of Allah. It was asked: Are the sun and the moon punished (يُعَذَّبَان)? He said: You have asked about something, so master it (فَأَتْقِنْهُ). Indeed, the sun and the moon are two signs (آيَتَان) among the signs of Allah. They run by His command, obedient to Him. Their light (ضَوْؤُهُمَا) is from the light (نُور) of His Throne (عَرْشِهِ), and their heat (حَرُّهُمَا) is from Hell (جَهَنَّم). When the Resurrection (الْقِيَامَة) occurs, their light will return to the Throne, and their heat will return to the Fire (النَّار), and there will be neither sun nor moon. He only meant them (the two individuals), may Allah curse them (لَعَنَهُمَا الله). Have not the people narrated that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Indeed, the sun and the moon are two lights in the Fire"? It was said: Yes. He said: Have you not heard the saying of the people: "So-and-so and so-and-so are the two suns (شَمْسَا) and the two lights (نُورُهُمَا) of this Ummah"? So they are in the Fire. By Allah, He did not mean anyone other than them. It was asked: النَّجْمُ وَالشَّجَرُ يَسْجُدَانِ (The star and the tree prostrate)? He said: The star (النَّجْم) is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And Allah has named him in another place, saying: وَالنَّجْمِ إِذَا هَوَى (By the star when it sets), and He said: وَعَلَامَاتٍ وَبِالنَّجْمِ هُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ (and landmarks, and by the star they are guided). So the landmarks (الْعَلَامَات) are the Successors (الْأَوْصِيَاء), and the star (النَّجْم) is the Messenger of Allah, the blessings of Allah be upon them. It was asked: يَسْجُدَانِ (They prostrate)? He said: They worship (يَعْبُدَان). And His saying: وَالسَّمَاءَ رَفَعَهَا وَوَضَعَ الْمِيزَانَ (And the heaven He raised, and set up the balance). He said: The heaven (السَّمَاء) is the Messenger of Allah, whom Allah raised to Him, and the balance (الْمِيزَان) is the Commander of the Faithful, the blessings of Allah be upon them both, whom He established for His creation. It was asked: أَلَّا تَطْغَوْا فِي الْمِيزَانِ (That you may not violate the balance)? He said: Do not disobey the Imam (الْإِمَام). It was asked: وَأَقِيمُوا الْوَزْنَ بِالْقِسْطِ (and maintain the weighing with justice)? He said: Establish the Imam (الْإِمَام) with justice (الْعَدْل). It was asked: وَلَا تُخْسِرُوا الْمِيزَانَ (and do not diminish the balance)? He said: Do not defraud the Imam (الْإِمَام) of his right (حَقَّهُ) and do not wrong him (تَظْلِمُوه). And His saying: وَالْأَرْضَ وَضَعَهَا لِلْأَنَامِ (And the earth He has set for the creatures). He said: For the people (لِلنَّاس). فِيهَا فَاكِهَةٌ وَالنَّخْلُ ذَاتُ الْأَكْمَامِ (In it are fruits, and the date-palms with sheaths). He said: The fruit of the date-palm grows large within the spathe (الْقِمْع) and then emerges from it. His saying: وَالْحَبُّ ذُو الْعَصْفِ وَالرَّيْحَانُ (and grain with the husk, and fragrant herbs). He said: The grain (الْحَبّ) is wheat (الْحِنْطَة), barley (الشَّعِير), and grains (الْحُبُوب); the husk (الْعَصْف) is straw (التِّبْن); and the fragrant herbs (الرَّيْحَان) are what is eaten from it.

(١٣) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ ** (13) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? Al-Qummi said: In the apparent sense (في الظّاهر), it is addressing the jinn and mankind (الجنّ والإنس), and in the inner sense (في الباطن), it is [addressing] so-and-so and so-and-so (فلان وفلان). And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: Allah said: "So which of the two bounties (النّعمتين) do you disbelieve in (تكفران): Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله or Ali عليه السلام?" And in al-Kafi, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): [Is it] the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله or the Successor (الوصيّ)? The exegetes (المفسّرون) have strained themselves to assign to الآلاء (bounties) in every instance of this surah a meaning different from its meaning in the other instance, which they derived from what preceded its mention. We have omitted that, being satisfied with what is contained in this narration (الحديث). And the reason for the repetition (التكرير) is similar to what passed in Surat al-Qamar.

(١٤) ** خَلَقَ الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ صَلْصالٍ كَالْفَخَّارِ ** (He created man from dry clay, like pottery.) Al-ṣalṣāl (الصلصال) is the dry clay (الطّين اليابس) which has a ringing sound (صلصلة), and al-fakhkhār (الفخّار) is pottery (الخزف). And Adam was indeed created from dust (تراب). He made it clay (طيناً), then black mud molded (حمأً مسنوناً), then dry clay (صلصالاً). Thus, there is no contradiction (تنافي) between what has been mentioned concerning each of them.

(١٥) وَخَلَقَ الْجَانَ ** (15) And He created the jinn from a smokeless flame of fire. refers to the father of the jinn (أبا الجنّ), as passed in Surat al-Hijr. مِنْ مارِجٍ ** (from a smokeless flame) means from the pure (صاف) part of the smoke (الدّخان) of fire (مِنْ نارٍ). This is a clarification (بيان) for mārij (مارج), for it originally refers to that which is agitated (لِلْمُضْطَرِب), derived from maraja (مرج) when it became agitated (اضطرب).

(١٦) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (16) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny?

(١٧) رَبُّ الْمَشْرِقَيْنِ وَرَبُّ الْمَغْرِبَيْنِ ** (17) Lord of the two easts and Lord of the two wests. refers to the two easts of winter and summer, and their two wests. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "The east of winter is separate, and the east of summer is separate. You only recognize that from the proximity and distance of the sun." He said: "As for His saying: بِرَبِّ الْمَشارِقِ وَالْمَغارِبِ ** (by the Lord of the easts and the wests), it is because it [the sun] has three hundred and sixty mansions (برجاً). It rises every day from one mansion and sets in another, and it does not return to it except in the following year (من قابل) on that same day." And al-Qummi [mentioned this] after interpreting the two [easts and wests] as we have interpreted them.

And it is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The two easts (المشرقين) are the Messenger of Allah and the Commander of the Faithful (may the prayers of Allah be upon them both), and the two wests (المغربين) are al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them both). He said: "And it applies to their likes (أمثالهما)."

(١٨) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (18) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (١٩) مَرَجَ الْبَحْرَيْنِ أرسل البحر العذاب والبحر الملح يَلْتَقِيانِ يتجاوزان. (19) He merged the two seas: He sent forth the sweet sea and the salty sea, they meet (they pass beyond one another). (٢٠) بَيْنَهُما بَرْزَخٌ حاجز من قدرة الله لا يَبْغِيانِ لا يبغي أحدهما على الآخر بالممازجة وإبطال الخاصّية. (20) Between them is a barrier (a partition) from the power of Allah, they do not transgress (neither of them transgresses against the other by mixing and nullifying the characteristic property). (٢١) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (21) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٢٢) يَخْرُجُ مِنْهُمَا اللُّؤْلُؤُ وَالْمَرْجانُ كبار الدرّ وصغاره وقيل المرجان الخرز الأحمر وقرئ يخرج على البناء للمفعول. (22) From them come forth the pearl and the coral: the large and small pearls. And it is said that al-marjān (المرجان) is the red bead. And it was recited yukhraju (يُخْرَجُ) using the passive construction.

وفي قرب الإسناد عن الصادق عن أبيه عن عليّ عليهم السلام : يَخْرُجُ مِنْهُمَا قال من ماء السّماء ومن ماء البحر فإذا أمطرت فتحت الاصداف أفواهها في البحر فيقع فيها من ماء المطر فتخلق الّلئالي الصّغيرة من القطرة الصغيرة واللّؤلؤ الكبيرة من القطرة الكبيرة. In Qurb al-Isnad, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from Ali (peace be upon them): Regarding ** يَخْرُجُ مِنْهُمَا ** (From them come forth), he said: "From the water of the sky and from the water of the sea. When it rains, the shells open their mouths in the sea, and some of the rainwater falls into them. Thus, the small pearls are created from the small drop, and the large pearls (اللّؤلؤ) from the large drop."

والقمّيّ عن الصادق عليه السلام قال : عليّ وفاطمة صلوات الله عليهما بحران عميقان لا يبغي أحدهما على صاحبه يَخْرُجُ مِنْهُمَا اللُّؤْلُؤُ وَالْمَرْجانُ قال الحسن والحسين عليهما السلام. And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: "Ali and Fatima (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both) are two deep seas; neither of them transgresses against the other. ** يَخْرُجُ مِنْهُمَا اللُّؤْلُؤُ وَالْمَرْجانُ ** (From them come forth the pearl and the coral)," he said, "are al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them)."

وفي المجمع عن سلمان الفارسيّ وسعيد بن جبير وسفيان الثوري : انّ الْبَحْرَيْنِ عليّ وفاطمة عليهما السلام والبرزخ محمّد صلّى الله عليه وآله واللُّؤْلُؤُ وَالْمَرْجانُ الحسن والحسين عليهما السلام. And in al-Majma', narrated from Salman al-Farsi, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and Sufyan al-Thawri: The two seas (الْبَحْرَيْنِ) are Ali and Fatima (peace be upon them), and the barrier (البرزخ) is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and ** اللُّؤْلُؤُ وَالْمَرْجانُ ** (the pearl and the coral) are al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them).

(٢٣) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (23) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٢٤) وَلَهُ الْجَوارِ ** The ships (السفن), which is the plural of (جارية), ** الْمُنْشَآتُ ** It was said: those with raised sails (المرفوعات الشراع). And it was recited with a kasrah on the shīn (الْمُنْشِئَاتُ), meaning: those that raise the sails فِي الْبَحْرِ كَالْأَعْلامِ ** like the mountains (كالجبال), which is the plural of (عَلَم), and it is the tall mountain. (24) To Him belong the ships, sailing on the sea like mountains. (٢٥) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (25) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٢٦) كُلُّ مَنْ عَلَيْها ** Everyone upon it, meaning everyone upon the face of the earth, is perishable (فَانٍ). (26) Everyone on it is bound to perish, (٢٧) وَيَبْقى وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ ذُو الْجَلالِ وَالْإِكْرامِ ** Possessor of absolute self-sufficiency (الاستغناء المطلق) and universal grace (الْفَضْل الْعَام). And that is because if you examine the aspects (جهات) of existing things and survey their faces (وجوهها), you will find them entirely perishable in their very essence, except the Face (وجه) of Allah, meaning: the aspect (الوجه) that pertains to His direction (جهته). And al-Qummi (said regarding) كُلُّ مَنْ عَلَيْها فانٍ : It was said: everyone upon the face of the earth. (And regarding) وَيَبْقى وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ : He said: the religion (دين) of your Lord. And from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "We are the Face (وجه) of Allah through Whom He is approached." And in al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): (Regarding) وَيَبْقى وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ : He said: "We are the Face (وجه) of Allah." And in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Jawad (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "And when Allah annihilates things, He annihilates the forms (الصّور) and the spellings (الهجاء), and He Who has always been Knowing (عالماً) will not cease and will not vanish." (27) but the Face of your Lord, the Possessor of majesty and honour, will remain.

(٢٨) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (28) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٢٩) يَسْئَلُهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** for they are needy of Him in their essences (ذواتهم) and their attributes (صفاتهم) and all that concerns them and is important to them. And what is meant by asking (السؤال) is that which indicates the need (الحاجة) to obtain the thing, whether it is verbal (نطقاً) or otherwise. كُلَّ يَوْمٍ هُوَ فِي شَأْنٍ ** such as the creation (احداث) of a wondrous thing (بديع) that was not previously so.

Narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a sermon (خطبة) narrated in الكافي and القمّيّ. He said: He gives life (يحيي) and causes death (يميت), and provides sustenance (يرزق), and increases (يزيد) and decreases (ينقص).

And in المجمع, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: regarding this verse, He said: It is part of His work (شأنه) that He forgives a sin (ذنباً), and relieves distress (كرباً), and raises up some people and lowers others. It is said that this is a refutation (ردٌّ) of the saying of the Jews that Allah does not decree anything on the Sabbath (يوم السّبت), or that He has finished the matter (الامر).

** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (30) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? ** سَنَفْرُغُ لَكُمْ أَيُّهَ الثَّقَلانِ ** (31) We shall attend to you, O you two burdens! And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء). It was said: meaning, We shall devote Ourselves (سنتجرّد) to your reckoning and your recompense, and that is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). For on that day, all the affairs (شئون) of creation cease, and only one affair remains, which is the recompense (الجزاء). So, that was made a 'freeness' (فراغاً) by way of similitude (على سبيل التمثيل).

And it was said: it is a threat (تهديد) borrowed from your saying to someone you threaten: "I shall attend to you (سأفرغ لك)," for the one who devotes himself (المتجرّد) to a thing is stronger over it and more earnest in it. And the two burdens (الثّقلان) are the Jinn (الجنّ) and mankind (الإنس).

And al-Qummi said: [The two burdens are] us (نحن) and the Book of Allah (كتاب الله). The proof (والدّليل) for that is the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "Indeed, I am leaving among you the two weighty things (الثّقلين): the Book of Allah (كتاب الله) and my progeny (عترتي), the People of my House (أهل بيتي)."

** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (32) So which of your Lord’s bounties do you both deny? ** يا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ أَنْ تَنْفُذُوا مِنْ أَقْطارِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (33) O company of jinn and humans! If you can pass through the confines of the heavens and the earth, If you are able to exit the sides (جوانب) of the heavens and the earth, fleeing from Allah, escaping His decree (قضائه), فَانْفُذُوا ** so exit. لا تَنْفُذُونَ ** You are not able to pass through (النّفوذ) إِلَّا بِسُلْطانٍ ** except with strength (قوّة) and subjugation (قهر). And how can that be for you? Or (it means): If you are able to pass through to know what is in the heavens and the earth, then pass through to know. But you will not pass through nor know except with a clear proof (بيّنة) established by Allah, upon which you ascend with your thoughts (أفكاركم). This is what has been said (كذا قيل).

And in al-Majma', it has come in the narration (الخبر): The creation will be surrounded by the angels and by a tongue (لسان) of fire. Then they will be called: يا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ (O company of jinn and humans! If you can) up to His saying: شُواظٌ مِنْ نارٍ. (a flame of fire.)

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, Allah will gather the servants upon a single plain (صعيد واحد). That is because He will reveal to the lowest heaven (السماء الدّنيا): "Descend with those who are in you." So the inhabitants of the lowest heaven will descend with twice the number of jinn, humans, and angels who are on the earth. Then the inhabitants of the second heaven will descend with twice the number of all of them. They will continue thus until the inhabitants of the seven heavens descend. Then the jinn and humans will be in seven enclosures (سرادقات) of angels. Then a caller (مناد) will call out: يا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ (O company of jinn and humans! If you can) [the rest of] the Ayah. Then they will look, and behold, they are surrounded by seven rings (اطواق) of angels.

And al-Qummi narrated something close to it. And it has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah regarding the Almighty's saying: هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ اللهُ فِي ظُلَلٍ مِنَ الْغَمامِ. (Are they waiting for anything but that Allah should come to them in the shadows of the clouds?)

(٣٤) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (34) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٣٥) يُرْسَلُ عَلَيْكُما شُواظٌ مِنْ نارٍ وَنُحاسٌ smoke (دخان) or molten brass (صفر مذاب) poured upon their heads. And it was read with the kasrah on the sīn (شِواظ), which is a dialect (لغة), and (نُحاس) with the jarr (بالجر). فَلا تَنْتَصِرانِ ** so you will not be able to prevent yourselves. (35) There will be sent against you both a flame of fire and smoke, (٣٦) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (36) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٣٧) فَإِذَا انْشَقَّتِ السَّماءُ فَكانَتْ وَرْدَةً *It was said (قيل): meaning, red like the rose (وردة) of the plant, or like the color of the ward (الورد) horse, which is the white one that tends towards redness, yellowness, or dustiness (الغبرة), and it changes in the seasons (الفصول). And al-wardah (الوردة) is the singular of al-ward (الورد*). So the sky on the Day of Resurrection was likened to that in the variation of its colors. كَالدِّهانِ (like grease/oil). It was said (قيل): like the oils (الدهان) which are poured one upon the other with different colors. And it was said (قيل): molten like oil (الدّهن), which is a name for what one anoints with, or it is the plural of duhn (دهن). And it was said (قيل): it is the red hide (الأديم الأحمر). (37) When the sky is split open, and becomes rosy like grease,

(٣٨) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (38) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٣٩) فَيَوْمَئِذٍ لا يُسْئَلُ عَنْ ذَنْبِهِ إِنْسٌ وَلا جَانٌ ** (39) On that day neither man nor jinn will be questioned about his sin. It was said that this is because they will be recognized by their marks (بسيماهم). And al-Qummi said: "From you (منكم)," meaning from the Shi'ah (من الشيعة). He said its meaning is: whoever took as his guardian (تولّى) the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and disassociated himself (تبرّأ) from His enemies, and believed in Allah, and held His lawful (حلاله) as lawful and His unlawful (حرامه) as unlawful, then committed sins (دخل في الذنوب) and did not repent in this world (في الدنيا), he will be punished for them (عذّب بها) in the intermediate realm (في البرزخ). And he will emerge on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) and will have no sin (ذنب) about which he will be questioned (يسئل عنه) on the Day of Resurrection.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said regarding this Ayah: Indeed, whoever believed in the truth (اعتقد الحقّ) then sinned (أذنب) and did not repent in this world (في الدنيا), he will be punished for it (عذّب عليه) in the intermediate realm (في البرزخ). And he will emerge on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) and will have no sin (ذنب) about which he will be questioned (يسئل عنه).

(٤٠) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (40) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٤١) يُعْرَفُ الْمُجْرِمُونَ بِسِيماهُمْ ** (41) The guilty will be recognized by their marks, (١) It was said: It is what covers them of gloom (الكآبة) and sorrow (الحزن). فَيُؤْخَذُ بِالنَّواصِي وَالْأَقْدامِ.** (41) and they will be seized by the forelocks and the feet.

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He asked some of his companions: "What do they say regarding this?" He (the companion) said: "They claim that Allah, the Exalted, will recognize the guilty by their marks (بِسِيماهُمْ) on the Day of Resurrection, and He will command concerning them, so they will be seized by their forelocks and their feet, and they will be cast into the Fire." So He (peace be upon him) said: "And how would He, the Blessed and Exalted, need to recognize a creation whom He originated and whom He created?" He (the companion) asked: "And what is that (وما ذاك)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "That refers to when our Qā'im (قائمنا) rises; Allah will grant him the mark (السّيماء). Then he will command concerning the disbelievers, and they will be seized by their forelocks and their feet, and then they will be struck repeatedly with the sword (يخبط بالسيف خبطاً)."

(42) ** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (42) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (43) ** هذِهِ جَهَنَّمُ الَّتِي يُكَذِّبُ بِهَا الْمُجْرِمُونَ. (43) This is Hell which the guilty deny. (44) ** يَطُوفُونَ بَيْنَها وَبَيْنَ حَمِيمٍ آنٍ ** (44) They will circulate between it and scalding water (ḥamīm ān). water that has reached the extreme limit (النهاية) in heat. And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): هذِهِ جَهَنَّمُ الَّتِي ** (This is Hell which) you two used to deny. Burn therein (both of you), so you will neither die in it nor live. And al-Qummi narrated that which is similar in meaning (ما في معناه).

(٤٥) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (45) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٤٦) وَلِمَنْ خافَ مَقامَ رَبِّهِ جَنَّتانِ. (46) But for him who fears the standing before his Lord there are two gardens. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): regarding this verse, He said: "Whoever knows that Allah sees him, hears what he says, and knows what He knows, whether good or evil, and this restrains him from ugly deeds, that is the one who خافَ مَقامَ رَبِّهِ وَنَهَى النَّفْسَ عَنِ الْهَوى (fears the standing before his Lord and restrains the soul from [evil] desire)." And in al-Faqih, in the prohibitions of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever is presented with an indecency (فاحشة) or a desire (شهوة) and avoids it out of fear of Allah the Almighty, Allah will forbid the Fire upon him, secure him from the Greatest Terror (الفزع الأكبر), and fulfill for him what He promised in His Book, in His Almighty saying: وَلِمَنْ خافَ مَقامَ رَبِّهِ جَنَّتانِ. (But for him who fears the standing before his Lord there are two gardens.)"

footnote (١) Meaning, by their sign (*علامتهم*), which is the blackness of the face and the blueness of the eyes.

(٤٧) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (47) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٤٨) ذَواتا أَفْنانٍ ** Possessing various kinds (ذواتا الوان) of bliss (النّعيم), or types (أنواع) of trees (الأشجار) and fruits (الثمار), being the plural of فنّ (kind/type), or branches (أغصان), being the plural of فنن (branchlet). The فنن is the small branch (الغصنة) that branches out (تتشعّب) from the main branch (فرع الشجر). Their specific mention (تخصيصها بالذكر) is because they are what bear leaves (تورق), yield fruit (تثمر), and extend shade (تمدّ الظلّ). (48) Possessing various kinds of foliage. (٤٩) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (49) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٥٠) فِيهِما عَيْنانِ تَجْرِيانِ. (50) In them are two flowing springs. (٥١) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (51) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٥٢) فِيهِما مِنْ كُلِّ فاكِهَةٍ زَوْجانِ ** Two types (صنفان): unfamiliar (غريب) and familiar (معهود), or fresh (رطب) and dry (يابس). (52) In them are two kinds of every fruit. (٥٣) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (53) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٥٤) مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلى فُرُشٍ بَطائِنُها مِنْ إِسْتَبْرَقٍ ** Made of thick brocade (من ديباج ثخين). What then do you imagine the outer layers (الظّهائر) to be? وَجَنَى الْجَنَّتَيْنِ دانٍ The fruit gathered from them (مجنيهما) is near (قريب); the seated person (القاعد) and the reclining person (المضطجع) can reach it. (54) Reclining on couches whose linings are of brocade, and the fruit of the two gardens is near at hand. (٥٥) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (55) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٥٦) فِيهِنَ ** In the Gardens (في الجنان) قاصِراتُ الطَّرْفِ ** Women (نساء) who have restrained their gazes (قصرن ابصارهنّ) upon their husbands (أزواجهنّ), desiring no one other than them. Al-Qummi said: The wide-eyed maidens (الحور العين) cause the gaze (الطرف) to be restrained from them (يقصر الطرف عنها) due to the brightness of their light (من ضوء نورها). لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنْسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلا جَانٌ ** No human (انس) has touched the human maidens (الانسيّات), nor has any jinn (جنّ) touched the jinn maidens (الجنيّات). وقرئ And it was recited with a dammah on the mīm (بضمّ الميم) [in يَطْمِثْهُنَّ]. (56) In them are maidens restraining their glances, whom no human or jinn has touched before them.

(٥٧) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (57) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٥٨) كَأَنَّهُنَّ الْياقُوتُ وَالْمَرْجانُ ** (58) As if they were rubies and corals. (This is) regarding the redness of the cheek (الوَجْنَة), the whiteness of the skin (البَشَرَة), and the purity of both of them.

And in al-Majma', in the narration (الحديث): Indeed, the woman from the people of Paradise—the marrow of her shin is seen from behind seventy garments (حُلَّة) of silk (حَرِير).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in a narration (حديث): (It is) similar to it, without His saying: "of silk (مِنْ حَرِير)."

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That which is in its meaning, with additions (زِيَادَات), and it has already passed in Surat al-Hajj.

(٥٩) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (59) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٦٠) هَلْ جَزاءُ الْإِحْسانِ إِلَّا الْإِحْسانُ (60) Is the requital of goodness anything but goodness? al-Qummi said: The requital of the one upon whom I have bestowed the favor of al-Ma'rifah (Gnosis) is nothing but Paradise (al-Jannah). And it was narrated in al-Tawhid from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and in al-Ilal from al-Hasan ibn Ali (peace be upon them both), from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace), who said: "Is the requital of the one who says لا إله إلّا الله (There is no god except Allah) anything but Paradise (al-Jannah)?" And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace): He recited this Ayah and then said: "Do you know what your Lord says?" They said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "Indeed, your Lord says: 'Is the requital of the one upon whom We have bestowed the favor of al-Tawhid (Monotheism) anything but Paradise (al-Jannah)?'" And from al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "This Ayah applies to the disbeliever (al-Kafir), the believer (al-Mu'min), the righteous (al-Barr), and the wicked (al-Fajir). Whoever has a kindness (ma'ruf) done to him, it is incumbent upon him to reciprocate it (yukafi bihi). Reciprocation (al-Mukafa'ah) is not merely doing as he did until you exceed it (tarba). For if you do exactly as he did, the merit (al-Fadl) of the initiation belongs to him."

(٦١) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (61) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٦٢)** وَمِنْ دُونِهِما جَنَّتانِ ** (62) And besides them are two other gardens. And besides those two gardens promised to those who fear the station of their Lord, there are two gardens for those beneath them.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: جَنَّتانِ ** (Two gardens) whose structures and what is within them are of silver, and جَنَّتانِ ** (two gardens) whose structures and what is within them are of gold.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Do not say that Paradise (الجنّة) is one, for Allah says: مِنْ دُونِهِما جَنَّتانِ ** (And besides them are two other gardens). And do not say that the degree (درجة) is one, for Allah says: بَعْضَهُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجاتٍ (some of them above others in degrees). Indeed, people are distinguished by their deeds (بالأعمال).

And narrated from Him عليه السلام: It was said to Him: People are astonished by us when we say that a group of people will exit the Fire (النّار) and enter Paradise (الجنّة), and they say to us: "Will they then be with the friends of Allah (أولياء الله) in Paradise?" He said: Indeed, Allah says: وَمِنْ دُونِهِما جَنَّتانِ (And besides them are two other gardens). No, by Allah, they will not be with the friends of Allah (أولياء الله).

And al-Qummi narrated from Him عليه السلام: That He was asked about His saying: وَمِنْ دُونِهِما جَنَّتانِ ** (And besides them are two other gardens). He said: They are two green gardens (خضراوان) in the world (الدنيا) from which the believers eat until the reckoning (الحساب) is finished.

(63) ** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (63) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (64) ** مُدْهامَّتان ** (Two gardens) intensely green (خضراوان), tending towards blackness (تضربان الى السّواد) due to the intensity of the greenness (من شدّة الخضرة). Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, regarding this verse, said: "What is between Mecca and Medina will be connected by date palms (نخلاً)." (65) ** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (65) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (66) ** فِيهِما عَيْنانِ نَضَّاخَتانِ ** In them are two gushing springs (فوّارتان). Al-Qummi, narrated from Him عليه السلام, said: "They gush forth (تفوران)." (67) ** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (67) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (68) ** فِيهِما فاكِهَةٌ وَنَخْلٌ وَرُمَّانٌ ** The conjunction of these two [date palms and pomegranates] upon the fruit (الفاكهة) is a clarification of their superiority (بياناً لفضلهما), for the fruit of the date palm (ثمرة النخل) is fruit (فاكهة) and sustenance (غذاء), and the pomegranate (الرمان) is fruit (فاكهة) and medicine (دواء). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "The fruit (الفاكهة) is one hundred and twenty types (لوناً), the master (سيّدها) of which is the pomegranate (الرمّان)." And narrated from Him عليه السلام: "Five of the fruits of Paradise are in this world: the al-Imlisi pomegranate (الرمّان الا مليسيّ), the apple (التفاح), the al-Shiyqan (والشيقان), the quince (والسفرجل), the al-Raziqi grape (والعنب الرّازقي), and the al-Mishan fresh date (والرّطب المشان)."

(٦٩) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (69) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٧٠) فِيهِنَّ خَيْراتٌ حِسانٌ. (70) In them are good and beautiful ones. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): Meaning, women who are good (خيرات) in character and beautiful (حسان) in countenance. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are the righteous (صوالح) believing women, the knowing ones (العارفات). And in al-Faqih, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The good and beautiful ones (الخيرات الحسان) are from the women of the people of this world, and they are more beautiful than the wide-eyed houris (الحور العين). And al-Qummi said: They are maidens (جوار) growing on the bank of al-Kawthar; whenever one of them is taken, another grows in its place. And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man's saying to another man, "May Allah reward you with good (جزاك الله خيراً)," what he means by it. He said: "Indeed, the best river in Paradise flows out from al-Kawthar, and al-Kawthar flows out from the Leg of the Throne (ساق العرش). Upon it are the dwellings (منازل) of the Successors (الأوصياء) and their followers (شيعتهم). On the banks (حافّتي) of that river are growing maidens (جواري نابتات); whenever one is plucked, another grows. They are named after that river, and that is His saying, the Almighty: فِيهِنَّ خَيْراتٌ حِسانٌ (In them are good and beautiful ones). So when a man says to his companion, 'May Allah reward you with good (جزاك الله خيراً),' he only means by that those dwellings which Allah has prepared for His chosen ones (صفوته) and the best (خيرته) of His creation."

(٧١) ** فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (71) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٧٢) ** حُورٌ مَقْصُوراتٌ فِي الْخِيامِ ** (72) Houris confined in pavilions, Confined/veiled women (مخدّرات).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Hour (الحور) are the fair-skinned (البيض), confined (المقصورات), veiled women (المخدّرات) in pavilions (خيام) of pearl, ruby, and coral. For every pavilion, there are four doors, and at every door, there are seventy youthful maidens (كاعباً) serving as guards (حجّاباً) for them. And every day, a bounty (كرامة) comes to them from Allah, Mighty is His remembrance, and Allah, Mighty and Majestic, gives glad tidings to the believers concerning them.

And al-Qummi (said regarding) حُورٌ مَقْصُوراتٌ ** (Houris confined): He said: The gaze (الطرف) is confined away from them. And it was said: They are confined in gaze (مقصورة الطّرف) to their husbands.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): The pavilion (الخيمة) is a single pearl, its length in the heaven is sixty miles. In every corner of it, there is a spouse (أهل) for the believer whom the others do not see.

And narrated from him (may Allah bless him and his progeny), he said: On the night I was taken on the Night Journey (ليلة اسري بي), I passed by a river whose banks were domes (قباب) of coral. I was called out to from it: "Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah!" I said: "O Gabriel, who are these?" He said: "These are maidens (جوار) from the Hour al-'Ayn (الحور العين). They sought permission from their Lord, Mighty and Majestic, to greet you with peace, and He granted them permission." So they said: "We are the eternal ones, so we shall not die. And we are the blissful ones, so we shall not despair. We are the wives (أزواج) of noble men." Then he (may Allah bless him and his progeny) recited: حُورٌ مَقْصُوراتٌ فِي الْخِيامِ (Houris confined in pavilions).

(٧٣) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (73) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٧٤) لَمْ يَطْمِثْهُنَّ إِنْسٌ قَبْلَهُمْ وَلا جَانٌ. (74) Neither human nor jinn have touched them before them. (٧٥) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (75) So which of your Lord’s bounties will you both deny? (٧٦) مُتَّكِئِينَ عَلى رَفْرَفٍ cushions (وسائد) or pillows (نمارق), which is the plural of رفرفة. And it was said that الرّفرف is a type of carpet (البسط) or the skirt (ذيل) of the tent, and it may be said of every wide garment (ثوب عريض) خُضْرٍ وَعَبْقَرِيٍّ حِسانٍ It was said: fine carpets (زرابيّ). And it was said: every embroidered garment (ثوب موشّى) is عبقريّ. And it was said that العبقريّ is attributed to عبقر, which the Arabs claim is the name of the land of the Jinn, so they attribute every wondrous thing (كلّ شيء عجيب) to it. What is intended by it is the genus (الجنس), and for that reason, it was described using the plural form. And it was read in the anomalous readings (الشّواذ): رفارف خضر وعباقريّ. And in al-Majma', He narrated it from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله.

(٧٧) فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ. (77) So which of your Lord’s bounties do you deny? (٧٨) تَبارَكَ اسْمُ رَبِّكَ تعالى اسمه فما ظنّك بذاته ذِي الْجَلالِ وَالْإِكْرامِ ** (78) Blessed is the Name of your Lord, Exalted is His Name, so what do you suppose concerning His Essence, ذِي الْجَلالِ وَالْإِكْرامِ (the Possessor of Majesty and Munificence)? وقرئ بالرّفع صفة للاسم. And it was recited with the rafa' (الرفع) (nominative case), as an attribute (صفة) for the Name (الاسم). القمّيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام : في هذه الآية قال نحن جلال الله وكرامته التي أكرم الله تبارك وتعالى العباد بطاعتنا ومحبّتنا. In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "We are the Majesty (جلال) of Allah and His Munificence (كرامته) with which Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, honored the servants through obedience to us and love for us." في الكافي عن جابر بن عبد الله قال : لمّا قرأ رسول الله صلّى الله عليه وآله الرّحمن على النّاس سكتوا فلم يقولوا شيئاً فقال رسول الله صلّى الله عليه وآله الجنّ كانوا أحسن جواباً منكم لمّا قرأت عليهم فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ قالوا لا بشيء من آلاءِ رَبّنا نكذّب. In al-Kafi, narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah, he said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله read al-Rahman to the people, they remained silent and did not say anything. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "The Jinn were better in response than you when I read to them فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ ** (So which of your Lord’s bounties do you deny?)." They said: "We do not deny any of the bounties (آلاء) of our Lord." في ثواب الأعمال عن الصادق عليه السلام : من قرأ سورة الرحمن فقال عند كل فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ لا بشيء من آلائِك ربّ اكذّب فان قرأها ليلاً ثمّ مات مات شهيداً وان قرأها نهاراً ثمّ مات مات شهيداً. In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Rahman and says at every فَبِأَيِّ آلاءِ رَبِّكُما تُكَذِّبانِ ** (So which of your Lord’s bounties do you deny?): 'O Lord, I do not deny anything of Your bounties (آلائِك),' then if he recites it at night and then dies, he dies a martyr (شهيداً), and if he recites it during the day and then dies, he dies a martyr (شهيداً)." وفي المجمع اخبار اخر في فضلها. And in al-Majma', there are other narrations (اخبار) concerning its merit (فضلها).

سُورة الواقعة مكّيّة

Surah Al-Waqi'ah (The Event) is Meccan

وقال ابن عبّاس وقتادة الّا آية منها نزلت بالمدينة وهي وَتَجْعَلُونَ ** Ibn Abbas and Qatadah said that all of it is Meccan, except for one verse which was revealed in Medina, and that is وَتَجْعَلُونَ **رِزْقَكُمْ أَنَّكُمْ تُكَذِّبُونَ

(82) and make it your provision that you deny [it]? وقيل الّا قوله ثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ وقوله أَفَبِهذَا الْحَدِيثِ نزلت في سفره إلى المدينة عدد آيها تسع وتسعون آية And it was said that all of it is Meccan, except for His saying ثُلَّةٌ مِنَ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (A multitude of the former generations) and His saying أَفَبِهذَا الْحَدِيثِ ** (Is it with this discourse), which were revealed during His journey to Medina. The number of its verses (آيها) is ninety-nine verses (آية*). بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful***
